title: Manifest → Brewfile + mise derivation
outcome: Manifest is now the derivation source for the Brewfile. New lib/generate.sh (panoply_gen_brewfile + filters --missing/--defaults, panoply_gen_mise, panoply_gen_brewfile_check) and bin/panoply-gen subcommand wired into the dispatcher; `panoply gen brewfile` reproduces config/Brewfile byte-for-byte and config/Brewfile is regenerated and re-headed as a GENERATED artifact. panoply gen is read-only/agent-safe (added to policy allow + descriptor cli.commands). mise generator emits a commented [tools] block for js/rust detect-only runtimes, honoring the 'manifest names the tool set, not versions' canon rule. panoply list already prints modules/tools (criterion met). Seeded bin/validate-substrate.sh as the cumulative E03 gate (S2/S4/S5 sections will go green as those stories land).
validation: GATE `panoply gen brewfile --check` PASS (exit 0, 'Brewfile matches manifest'). `--missing --defaults` emits exactly the 5 missing defaults (zsh-autosuggestions, zsh-syntax-highlighting, tealdeer, btop, dua-cli). PANOPLY_AGENT=1 panoply gen runs (read-only). shellcheck --severity=warning CLEAN (only SC1091/SC2015 info, at parity with existing panoply-doctor + validators).
decisions: config/Brewfile becomes a generated artifact (header says GENERATED; regenerate via panoply gen brewfile); --check enforces manifest==Brewfile as the gate
mise generator stays informational/commented (no --check) because runtime versions are mise/proto's job, not the manifest's, per the native-toolchain canon
validate-substrate.sh authored once as the cumulative E03 gate rather than per-story validators; only its brewfile section is expected to pass at S1
next: E03.S2 — doctor --json, panoply env redesign, bootstrap hardening
token_note: loaded 1 story block + 1 canon ref + STATE/ledger tail; generators print compact stdout (no install logs); --check is one-line PASS/FAIL
